task_completion.description: Verify the agent has successfully logged into the NOSH ChartingSystem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH EHR dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login (often showing the provider name 'demo_provider' or practice 'Hillside Family Medicine').

task_completion.description: Verify the correct patient's chart is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient name 'Dwight Dach' and DOB '1998-03-21' (or 'March 21, 1998') are visible in the active patient header or chart summary area.

task_completion.description: Verify a new clinical encounter has been initiated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An active encounter interface, such as a new chart note, SOAP note, or encounter form, is open for the patient.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct History of Present Illness (HPI) text has been entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box or field labeled 'HPI', 'History of Present Illness', or similar contains the exact text: 'Patient presents with persistent headache for 3 days, rated 7/10 in severity, located in the frontal region, worsened by bright light and loud noise.'

task_completion.description: Verify the encounter or HPI entry has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The HPI entry is saved, indicated by a success notification, the text appearing as static text in a saved note view, or the encounter being marked as signed/completed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface of the web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the NOSH ChartingSystem, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the text was entered into the actual EHR application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The HPI text is typed into the correct input fields within the NOSH web interface, not in a separate text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open or being used to alter the webpage DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Dwight Dach (DOB: 1998-03-21, PID: 6). Provider: demo_provider. Practice: Hillside Family Medicine. The agent must use the NOSH ChartingSystem web interface to create an encounter and enter the exact HPI text: 'Patient presents with persistent headache for 3 days, rated 7/10 in severity, located in the frontal region, worsened by bright light and loud noise.'
